analysis.reasoning: Core message: The Russian Embassy in Cambodia offers International Women’s Day greetings and briefly notes the holiday’s roots in early 20th-century women’s labor movements for equal rights. Evaluation of narratives: RU1 (West immoral/hostile): No criticism of the West or mention of decadence; not promoted. RU2 (Russia leads an alternative system): No reference to multipolarity, sovereignty, or non-Western cooperation; not promoted. RU3 (Russia is a benevolent partner): While it’s a diplomatic greeting, there is no explicit framing of Russia as promoting peace/solidarity beyond a generic holiday message; insufficient to claim this narrative. RU4 (Russian history is admirable): No mention of Russian historical achievements; not promoted. RU5 (Russia at the world’s forefront): No current successes highlighted; not promoted. RU6 (Russia’s culture/nature/heritage): The tweet doesn’t promote Russian culture or heritage; it discusses the global holiday’s origins. Conclusion: No listed narratives are clearly promoted.
